name: Generic Purple Gradient
alternative: Use brand-specific gradient tokens or subtle single-hue gradients with CSS variables
description: Purple-to-pink gradients are the most overused AI-generated design pattern. They signal zero design intent and make every app look identical.

name: Hardcoded White Background
alternative: Use bg-background or bg-card semantic tokens from your design system
description: Hardcoded bg-white without dark: variant breaks dark mode and couples your UI to a specific theme. Always use semantic color tokens.

name: Hardcoded Black Text
alternative: Use text-foreground or text-muted-foreground semantic tokens
description: Hardcoded text-black without dark: variant breaks dark mode and ignores accessibility contrast requirements across themes.

name: Neon on Light Background
alternative: Use darker shade variants (600-700) for text on light backgrounds or reserve neon for dark themes only
description: Neon/bright accent colors on light backgrounds create poor contrast ratios and strain readability.

name: Rainbow Color Overload
alternative: Limit palette to 2-3 color families max per component and derive shades from CSS custom properties
description: Using 5+ distinct color families in one component creates visual chaos and undermines brand coherence.

name: Low Contrast Text
alternative: Use text-muted-foreground (min 4.5:1 contrast) or check with WCAG contrast checker before shipping
description: Light gray text on white or near-white backgrounds fails WCAG AA contrast requirements and is unreadable for many users.

name: Default Blue-500
alternative: Define primary color as a CSS variable (--primary) and use bg-primary/text-primary tokens
description: Using raw Tailwind blue-500 instead of semantic tokens makes theming impossible and screams default template.

name: No Dark Mode Support
alternative: Add dark: variants for all color utilities or use semantic tokens that auto-adapt
description: Components without any dark: variants break in dark mode and show you skipped half the design work.

name: Generic Inter Only
alternative: Configure a distinctive display font pairing in tailwind.config and use font-heading/font-body tokens
description: Inter is the default AI-generated font choice. Add at least a custom display/heading font to create visual identity.

name: Excessive Bold Text
alternative: Use font-bold sparingly for emphasis only; rely on font-size and color for hierarchy instead
description: When everything is bold nothing stands out. Over-bolding flattens visual hierarchy and fatigues readers.

name: Tiny Important Text
alternative: Use text-sm minimum for actionable or critical information; reserve text-xs for tertiary metadata only
description: Important information like prices or error messages in text-xs is hard to read and signals low importance to users.

name: Missing Heading Hierarchy
alternative: Always start with h1 then h2 before using h3+; use a HeadingLevel context provider if needed
description: Skipping h1/h2 and jumping to h3/h4 without an h1/h2 present breaks document outline for screen readers and harms SEO.

name: Inconsistent Font Sizes
alternative: Define text size tokens per role (body/caption/label) in your design system and apply consistently
description: Same-role elements with different text sizes create visual noise and undermine design system consistency.

name: Wall of Text
alternative: Add max-w-prose (65ch) to text containers and break content into digestible paragraphs
description: Long paragraphs without max-w-prose width constraints are unreadable. Limit line length to 45-75 characters.

name: System Font Only
alternative: Add a custom font in next/font and extend fontFamily in tailwind.config.ts
description: Relying solely on font-sans with no custom font configuration results in a generic look indistinguishable from any template.

name: Symmetric Hero Layout
alternative: Try asymmetric layouts (60/40 split) or full-bleed hero with overlapping elements for more visual interest
description: The 50/50 text-left image-right hero is the most cliched AI layout. Break symmetry to create visual interest.

name: Everything Centered
alternative: Left-align body text and use center only for headings or CTA sections; vary alignment for hierarchy
description: Centering every element creates a monotonous layout with poor readability for body text.

name: No Visual Hierarchy
alternative: Vary spacing (gap-2/gap-6/gap-12) and element sizes to create clear primary/secondary/tertiary hierarchy
description: Uniform spacing and sizing across all elements makes nothing stand out. Vary scale to guide the eye.

name: Padding Inconsistency
alternative: Define spacing tokens (sm/md/lg) and use consistent padding for same-level container elements
description: Mixing p-4 p-6 p-8 on sibling elements at the same hierarchy level creates visual jitter and feels unpolished.

name: No Responsive Breakpoints
alternative: Add md: and lg: breakpoint variants for grid columns and padding; test at 375px/768px/1280px
description: Grid layouts without sm:/md:/lg: responsive breakpoints break on mobile and show the layout was only tested at one size.

name: Sidebar Too Wide
alternative: Keep sidebar width at w-64 (256px) max and use collapsible sidebar for extra content
description: Sidebars wider than 288px steal too much horizontal space from main content on standard laptop screens.

name: Content Too Narrow
alternative: Use max-w-4xl or max-w-6xl for main content areas; reserve max-w-sm for cards or modals only
description: Main content constrained to max-w-sm (384px) or smaller wastes screen space and forces excessive scrolling.

name: Grid Overuse
alternative: Use flex for single-row layouts; reserve CSS grid for actual 2D grid patterns
description: Using grid-cols-1 for single-column layouts adds unnecessary complexity. Use flex-col or simple block layout.

name: Transition All Abuse
alternative: Use transition-colors or transition-opacity or transition-transform for the specific property animating
description: transition-all animates every CSS property including layout-triggering ones causing janky performance.

name: Too Many Animations
alternative: Limit to 1-2 animations per viewport; use animation only to convey meaning not decoration
description: Pages with 5+ simultaneous animations feel chaotic and distract from content. Animate with purpose.

name: No Reduced Motion Support
alternative: Wrap animations in motion-safe: or add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) fallbacks
description: Animations without prefers-reduced-motion check cause motion sickness for vestibular disorder users. Verify motion-reduce is paired.

name: Jarring Entrance Animation
alternative: Use duration-200 minimum for entrance animations; 300-500ms feels natural for most UI transitions
description: Ultra-fast entrance animations (75-100ms) appear as jarring flashes rather than smooth transitions.

name: Lorem Ipsum Left In
alternative: Replace with real or realistic content; use faker/seed data for development placeholders
description: Lorem ipsum in production signals incomplete work and prevents testing real content layout edge cases.

name: Missing Empty State
alternative: Add explicit empty state UI with illustration and CTA when data array is empty (items.length === 0)
description: Data lists using .map() without a corresponding empty state check show blank voids that confuse users.

name: No Loading State
alternative: Add Skeleton components or loading spinners while async data is in flight; use Suspense boundaries
description: Async data fetching calls without corresponding loading/skeleton state cause layout shifts and leave users staring at empty screens.
